memory_seeds.description: I felt a lot of anxiety after the LA fires, which made it feel very imperative to do something about fire mitigation.
I am doing the thing to mitigate ignition around my home by removing plants within five feet, which has a dramatic effect.
I find the emotional experience of ripping out my lush garden to be very difficult, as we moved up here to be closer to nature.
I was banned from posting to our Firewise community's Google group by Jennifer after I pushed for the Ember initiative.
My insurance policy was canceled last year by the company we'd been with for over 30 years, despite my offer to pay triple.
I learned that under the Fair Plan, my $3 million policy would only cover $1 million because they discounted it based on my 36-year-old clay tile roof.
I now have to pay about five times what I was paying before for insurance from a company not regulated by the California commissioner.
I organized three cooking classes as fundraisers and am encouraging the church to host monthly potlucks to bring the community together for firewise.
I advocate for the city to set up preventative shelters for voluntary evacuations, like cooling centers, because people won't leave if they have nowhere to go.
I remember seeing a Nextdoor post from Rhonda asking why people move to the Berkeley Hills if they hate plants so much, which I found off-putting and an assumption about motives.
